pullquote_provenance.speaker: Teamed Client Operations
pullquote_provenance.note: Anonymous-team voice per memory rule (feedback_pullquote_provenance): Teamed Client Operations is the canonical anonymisation when no MI extraction supports a named-individual quote. Quote drafts the in-house view on PLAWA frontload-versus-accrue, the six onboarding decisions, and the Chicago overlay; no MI provenance required because no individual is named, no cite tag included.

factual_uncertainty_flags.note: 16-day cure period auto-expires 1 July 2026. Calendar entry recommended. After expiry the only remedy is the up-to-3x civil action.

Chicago minimum wage ($16.60 through 30 June 2026) drives the floor pay rate during Chicago paid-leave use. The 1 July 2026 rate is not yet published; re-verify in late Q2 2026 once the Office of Labor Standards announcement lands.
Page cites the three-band ladder at 820 ILCS 180/20(a). Confirm against current IDOL VESSA guidance and any 2025-2026 amendments to the Act before relying on a specific band for a client matter.

Family Bereavement Leave Act (820 ILCS 154) and School Visitation Rights Act (820 ILCS 147) thresholds cited at 50+ employer per current statute summaries. Verify exact employee-count test and qualifying-event list against the latest IDOL guidance before client-specific advice.

IDOL guidance on whether separately-tracked PLAWA can lapse without payout when employer otherwise has a paid-vacation policy continues to mature. The combined-PTO payout rule under 820 ILCS 115 is settled; the PLAWA-as-its-own-line carve-out has FAQ support but should be re-checked against the latest IDOL FAQ before relying on the lapse mechanic.

scheduled_data_update.action: Update page to remove cure-period language after 1 July 2026 auto-expiry; verify whether OLS extends or supersedes the rule.
Re-verify Chicago minimum wage after 1 July 2026 reset (currently $16.60; could rise per CPI ≤ 2.5% rule). Update the Chicago Ordinance pay-rate table accordingly.
Re-check IDOL PLAWA FAQ for any 2026 clarifications on combined-PTO tracking, carry-over arithmetic, and frontload reset mechanics.
